← All IRC handicap examples

IRC — additive scoring penalty (SCP) applies in a handicap fleet

Additive penalties (ZFP/SCP/DPI under RRS 44.3(c)) apply to finishers in handicap fleets exactly as in scratch fleets: the penalty is the stated % of the DNF score, rounded to the nearest tenth of a point (0.05 up), added to the corrected-time finishing-place score, and capped at the DNF score. This mirrors the scratch fixture codes/05-scp-penalty so the two paths are demonstrably consistent. All five boats carry TCC 1.000, so corrected-time order equals finishing order. Five competitors, N=5, DNF score = 6. Alice 1st + SCP (default 20%) -> 1 + 0.20 x 6 = 1 + 1.2 = 2.2 Bob 2nd + SCP (override 30%) -> 2 + 0.30 x 6 = 2 + 1.8 = 3.8 Carol 3rd -> 3 Dave 4th -> 4 Eve 5th -> 5

Scoring system: IRC

Race arithmetic

Gun time: 14:00:00
RankNameSail #TCCFinish timeETTCFCTPoints
1 Alice 1 1.000 15:00:00 60m 00s 1.0000 60m 00s 1
2 Bob 2 1.000 15:01:00 61m 00s 1.0000 61m 00s 2
3 Carol 3 1.000 15:02:00 62m 00s 1.0000 62m 00s 3
4 Dave 4 1.000 15:03:00 63m 00s 1.0000 63m 00s 4
5 Eve 5 1.000 15:04:00 64m 00s 1.0000 64m 00s 5

Series standings

RankNameSail #R1TotalNet
1 Alice 1 2.2 2.2 2.2
2 Carol 3 3 3 3
3 Bob 2 3.8 3.8 3.8
4 Dave 4 4 4 4
5 Eve 5 5 5 5
sailscoring.ie
